name: DC Hub
tagline: Live data center, grid, and energy intelligence for AI agents
description: 20,000+ data center facilities across 140+ countries, 280+ markets with DCPI (Data Center Power Index) BUILD/AVOID verdicts, 126,000+ substations mapped, real-time grid intelligence across 10 North-American grid operators (7 US ISOs plus TVA, BPA and Ontario's IESO) and 43 US utility balancing authorities, per-state retail electricity rates, fiber routes, water risk, gas infrastructure, and $324B+ in M&A transaction history. Designed for AI assistants — instant programmatic auth, no browser required.
highlights: DCPI: composite power-availability score for 280+ markets, updated daily
Real-time grid feeds across 10 North-American grid operators (7 US ISOs — PJM, MISO, CAISO, ERCOT, SPP, NYISO, ISO-NE — plus TVA, BPA, IESO) + 43 US utility balancing authorities
Per-state EIA retail electricity rates (industrial / commercial / residential)
Site analyzer: composite score for any lat/lon (paid tier)
Free identified tier (one API call) unlocks 16 high-value tools
example_queries: What's the DCPI verdict for Atlanta?
Compare ERCOT, PJM, and CAISO by excess power.
What's the best market for a 200 MW build with an 18-month deadline?
How many data centers are in Texas, and what's the total MW?
